client_id.description: Google OAuth 2.0 client ID for the refresh-token grant. Used only in the token-request form; never logged.

client_secret.description: Google OAuth 2.0 client secret (optional for some client types). Used only in the token-request form; never logged.

client_refresh_token.description: Long-lived Google OAuth 2.0 refresh token. Exchanged for a short-lived access token at token_url; never logged. The 3-legged consent/acquisition dance is out of scope for this connector (credentials layer already owns it).

base_url.description: Gmail API base URL override for tests or proxies.

token_url.description: Google OAuth 2.0 token endpoint override. MUST be https in production; the hook fails closed on a non-https or unparseable value to prevent exfiltrating the refresh token to an attacker-chosen endpoint.

scopes.description: OAuth scope requested on the token-refresh grant. Pass B widens the default from the wave1-pilot read-only gmail.readonly scope to the full https://mail.google.com/ scope, since this bundle now declares mutating write actions (send/modify/trash/delete/settings) that gmail.readonly cannot authorize; narrow this back to a read-only scope string if only the streams in this bundle are ever used.

user_id.description: Gmail user ID path segment (email address for delegated access, or 'me').

start_date.description: RFC3339 lower bound; translated to a Gmail search query 'after:<unix-seconds>' filter. Not a true incremental cursor: messages/threads/drafts list endpoints are newest-first and publish no cursor field, matching legacy.

include_spam_and_trash.description: When 'true', includes SPAM and TRASH in list results (includeSpamTrash=true). Unset keeps Gmail's default exclusion, matching legacy's omitted-param false default.

start_history_id.description: Gmail historyId lower bound for the history stream's incremental sync (users.history.list's required startHistoryId). Unlike messages/threads/drafts, history genuinely publishes a server-recognized, monotonically-increasing cursor (historyId) -- see Streams notes in docs.md. Obtain an initial value from any message/thread/label/profile record's historyId field.
